aids.title: String Immutability - SCHL
aids.content: **S**ecurity, **C**aching, **H**ashcode consistency, **L**oading classes safely. SCHL - 'Strings Can't Help but Lock' - reasons why Java Strings are immutable.

aids.title: String Pool vs new Keyword
aids.content: String pool is like a library - if the book exists, you get a reference to the same copy. Using 'new' is like printing a fresh copy even if the library already has one.
aids.forRemembering: Difference between literal and new String creation

aids.title: == vs equals() Rule
aids.content: == checks if two references point to the SAME object (address comparison). equals() checks if two strings have the SAME content. Always use equals() for string content comparison!
aids.forRemembering: When to use == vs equals() for strings

aids.title: CLIPS - Common String Methods
aids.content: **C**harAt, **L**ength, **I**ndexOf, **P**ubstring (substring), **S**plit. CLIPS - the essential String methods you must know.

aids.title: StringBuilder vs StringBuffer
aids.content: StringBuilder is faster (not synchronized, single-threaded). StringBuffer is thread-safe (synchronized, multi-threaded). Remember: 'Builder is Better for speed, Buffer is for thread safety.'
aids.forRemembering: When to use StringBuilder vs StringBuffer
